perturbation.original_text: Each party undertakes irrevocably and unconditionally: a. To ensure that all aspects of each transaction remain confidential. b. Not to disclose, either verbally or in written form, any knowledge that it may obtain at any time in the future, be it either implicit or <PAGE> implied, with respect to the implementation of any proposed transaction. c. Not to circumvent or attempt to circumvent the other. d. Not to disclose to third parties the names, addresses, fax and telephone coordinates of any contact/client revealed by one party to the other. e. Not to enter into direct or indirect negotiations with any other's contacts/clients. f. Not to show, deliver, or cause to be seen, any documents, papers, correspondence, memoranda or copies of such to other than person(s) or entities of any kind, except to those whom are required to maintain confidentiality such as an attorney or tax advisor. g. Each Party shall endeavor to ensure that any of its officers, employees, agents, representatives or associates who, by virtue of their duties may receive the type of information described in this Agreement, are fully obligated to respect the spirit and terms of this Agreements in the same way as each Party. Each Party shall undertakes to have those officers, employees, agents, representatives or associates acknowledge their obligation by Countersigning a copy of this Agreement, thereby binding them to honor the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each individual undertakes irrevocably and unconditionally: a. To ensure that all aspects of each transaction remain confidential. b. Not to disclose, either verbally or in written form, any knowledge that it may obtain at any time in the future, be it either implicit or <PAGE> implied, with respect to the implementation of any proposed transaction. c. Not to circumvent or attempt to circumvent the other. d. Not to disclose to third parties the names, addresses, fax and telephone coordinates of any contact/client revealed by one party to the other. e. Not to enter into direct or indirect negotiations with any other's contacts/clients. f. Not to show, deliver, or cause to be seen, any documents, papers, correspondence, memoranda or copies of such to other than person(s) or entities of any kind, except to those whom are required to maintain confidentiality such as an attorney or tax advisor. g. Each Party shall endeavor to ensure that any of its officers, employees, agents, representatives or associates who, by virtue of their duties may receive the type of information described in this Agreement, are fully obligated to respect the spirit and terms of this Agreements in the same way as each Party. Each Party shall undertakes to have those officers, employees, agents, representatives or associates acknowledge their obligation by Countersigning a copy of this Agreement, thereby binding them to honor the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing "party" with "individual" in the opening sentence of Section 3 creates a contradiction with subsection 'g,' which refers to "Each Party" ensuring obligations of its officers, employees, etc. The term "individual" implies a personal obligation, potentially excluding the corporate entity (Gateway Venture Holdings, Inc.) from directly being bound by subsections a-f. Subsection 'g' then contradicts this by referring back to "Each Party".
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party shall endeavor to ensure that any of its officers, employees, agents, representatives or associates who, by virtue of their duties may receive the type of information described in this Agreement, are fully obligated to respect the spirit and terms of this Agreements in the same way as each Party. Each Party shall undertakes to have those officers, employees, agents, representatives or associates acknowledge their obligation by Countersigning a copy of this Agreement, thereby binding them to honor the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from "party" to "individual" in the opening sentence introduces ambiguity regarding who is bound by the agreement. The contradicted text refers to "Each Party" ensuring obligations, but if the agreement initially binds only "individuals," it's unclear how a party (like a corporation) can ensure the obligations of its officers, as subsection g requires. This creates a contradiction because the contradicted text relies on 'Each Party' being the obligated entity from the outset, which is undermined by changing the initial obligation to apply to individuals.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement has been entered into by each Party acting on its own free will and judgment and shall be binding on the Parties, their heirs or successors, administrators, and assignees.
perturbation.changed_text: This Contract has been entered into by each Party acting on its own free will and judgment and shall be binding on the Parties, their heirs or successors, administrators, and assignees.
perturbation.explanation: Switching 'Agreement' to 'Contract' at the beginning of Section 5 while maintaining 'Agreement' throughout the rest of the section creates ambiguity. It's unclear whether 'Contract' is meant to be a synonym for 'Agreement' or refers to a different document or understanding. This can lead to disputes over whether all provisions apply equally. For example, 'this Contract' being binding versus 'the Parties' obligations of the agreement in previous sections.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Contract has been entered into by each Party acting on its own free will and judgment and shall be binding on the Parties, their heirs or successors, administrators, and assignees.
perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation specifies that changing 'Agreement' to 'Contract' in the changed text, while maintaining 'Agreement' throughout the rest of the section, creates ambiguity. The contradicted text uses 'Contract'. Because of the switch, it's unclear if 'Contract' is meant to be a synonym for 'Agreement' or a different document. This aligns with the Misaligned Terminology definition, which highlights contradictions arising from the interchangeable use of terms without clear definition, leading to conflicting obligations. The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text because now in the agreement itself, one section refers to 'Agreement' and the other to 'Contract', when they should both be referring to the same thing, as there isn't any definition provided to consider them different. This creates an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Any dispute or controversy arising out of or relating to any interpretation, construction, performance, or breach of this Agreement shall be resolved exclusively by binding arbitration in Las Vegas, Nevada, in accordance with the rules then in effect of the American Arbitration Association, The arbitrator(s) may grant injunctions or other relief in such dispute or controversy. The decision of the arbitrator(s) shall be final, conclusive, and binding on the parties to the arbitration. Judgment may be entered on the arbitrator's decision in any court having jurisdiction. The parties to the arbitration shall each pay an equal share of the costs and expenses of such <PAGE> arbitration, and each of them shall separately pay their counsel fees and expenses.
perturbation.changed_text: Any dispute or controversy arising out of or relating to any interpretation, construction, performance, or breach of this understanding shall be resolved exclusively by binding arbitration in Las Vegas, Nevada, in accordance with the rules then in effect of the American Arbitration Association, The arbitrator(s) may grant injunctions or other relief in such dispute or controversy. The decision of the arbitrator(s) shall be final, conclusive, and binding on the parties to the arbitration. Judgment may be entered on the arbitrator's decision in any court having jurisdiction. The parties to the arbitration shall each pay an equal share of the costs and expenses of such <PAGE> arbitration, and each of them shall separately pay their counsel fees and expenses.
perturbation.explanation: By substituting "Agreement" with "understanding" within Section 6, which governs dispute resolution, while maintaining the term "arbitration" and 'the parties to the arbitration' later in the same section, creates ambiguity regarding the scope of arbitration. It is now unclear if disputes arising from aspects of the overall "Agreement" that are not part of the parties' "understanding" are subject to arbitration. The scope is limited to "understanding" but later still refers to 'the parties to the arbitration'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any dispute or controversy arising out of or relating to any interpretation, construction, performance, or breach of this understanding shall be resolved exclusively by binding arbitration in Las Vegas, Nevada, in accordance with the rules then in effect of the American Arbitration Association, The arbitrator(s) may grant injunctions or other relief in such dispute or controversy. The decision of the arbitrator(s) shall be final, conclusive, and binding on the parties to the arbitration. Judgment may be entered on the arbitrator's decision in any court having jurisdiction. The parties to the arbitration shall each pay an equal share of the costs and expenses of such <PAGE> arbitration, and each of them shall separately pay their counsel fees and expenses.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Agreement" to "understanding" creates a contradiction. The original text refers to disputes arising from the entire "Agreement." The changed text limits dispute resolution to the parties' "understanding," potentially excluding other aspects of the Agreement from mandatory arbitration while still referring to 'the parties to the arbitration'. This introduces ambiguity and inconsistency, as the scope of arbitration is now unclear, creating a strong in-text contradiction because it narrows the original scope. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, reinforcing that the edited language now exists within the contract causing conflict.
